title: What's in Its Pockets (d20)
summary: A quick-reach loot table for any humanoid the party stops to search — most of it mundane, some of it asking a question the bearer can no longer answer.
body: A quick-reach loot table for any humanoid the party stops to search — most of it mundane, some of it asking a question the bearer can no longer answer.
1. Three copper coins and a smooth river stone, warm from body heat.
2. A folded letter, sealed but never sent, addressed to someone named "Mira."
3. Half a hardtack biscuit wrapped in a scrap of oilcloth.
4. A small knife, blade notched, handle wrapped in yellow cord.
5. Seven teeth. Not the bearer's.
6. A prayer token — wrong faith for this region.
7. A key on a cord, iron, unlabelled.
8. Two mismatched dice, both weighted the same way.
9. A scrap of map, edges burned, showing a coastline with an X.
10. A glass eye, brown, wrapped in soft cloth.
11. A pouch of dried herbs — culinary, harmless, and a long way from where they grow.
12. Twelve silver coins and one of pure gold, older-minted, the face unfamiliar.
13. A child's toy carved from bone. The cuts are fresh.
14. A folded wanted notice. The face on it is the party member who searched them.
15. A vial of dark liquid, stoppered tight, no label — not quite the same colour twice.
16. A small iron box, locked, no key. It is ticking.
17. A ring of hair woven tight, still on a severed finger.
18. A letter of safe passage, signed by someone who died three years ago.
19. A crude sketch of the nearest settlement — exits marked, guards noted.
20. Nothing. The pockets are sewn shut from the inside.
